questions.question: What does margin: 10px 20px; mean?
questions.explanation: With 2 values: first = top/bottom, second = left/right.

questions.question: What is margin collapsing?
options: Vertical margins combine to the larger value
questions.explanation: When two vertical margins meet, they collapse into a single margin equal to the larger value.

questions.question: How do you center a block element horizontally?
questions.explanation: margin: 0 auto centers a block element horizontally when it has a defined width.

questions.question: Can margins have negative values?
questions.explanation: Margins can be negative, pulling elements in opposite directions.

questions.question: What is the correct order of values in the margin shorthand with 4 values?
questions.explanation: The 4-value margin shorthand follows clockwise order: Top, Right, Bottom, Left (TRBL).

questions.question: Which elements does margin collapsing affect?
questions.explanation: Margin collapsing only affects vertical margins (top/bottom) on block elements in normal flow.

questions.question: How can you prevent margin collapsing between parent and child?
options: Use display: flex on the parent
questions.explanation: Using display: flex or display: grid on the parent prevents margin collapsing by creating a new formatting context.

questions.question: What happens to top and bottom margins on inline elements?
options: They are applied but don't affect layout
questions.explanation: Inline elements ignore top and bottom margins for layout purposes, though the margins are technically applied.

questions.question: What is the default margin value for all elements?
questions.explanation: The default margin value in CSS is 0 for all elements, though browsers apply default styles with various margins.

questions.question: What does margin-left: auto do on a block element with a set width?
options: Pushes the element to the left
Pushes the element to the right
questions.explanation: margin-left: auto pushes a block element to the right by absorbing all available space on the left.

questions.question: What does margin: 10px 20px 30px; mean?
questions.explanation: With 3 values: top = 10px, left/right = 20px, bottom = 30px.

questions.question: Are percentage margins calculated relative to the element's width or height?
options: Width for left/right, height for top/bottom
The containing block's width for all directions
The element's own width for all directions
The containing block's height for all directions
questions.explanation: Percentage margins are calculated relative to the WIDTH of the containing block for all directions, including top and bottom.

questions.question: What is the best practice for spacing in flexbox or grid layouts?
questions.explanation: The gap property is specifically designed for spacing between flex/grid children and should be preferred over margins.

questions.question: Can margin: auto center an element vertically?
options: No, auto margins only center horizontally on block elements with a set width
Yes, but only with box-sizing: border-box
No, vertical centering requires negative margins
questions.explanation: margin: auto only centers horizontally on block elements with a set width. Vertical centering requires flexbox, grid, or other techniques.

questions.question: What is a common use case for negative margins?
options: Creating overlapping or pulled-out visual effects
questions.explanation: Negative margins are commonly used for overlapping hero sections, pulling elements out of containers, and other visual effects.

questions.question: What does display: flow-root do in relation to margins?
options: Creates a new block formatting context, preventing margin collapse
Makes margins work on inline elements
questions.explanation: display: flow-root creates a new block formatting context that prevents margin collapse with no side effects.

questions.question: What is the advantage of one-direction margin patterns (e.g., only margin-top)?
options: They create more predictable and maintainable spacing
They work better with inline elements
questions.explanation: One-direction margins (like margin-top on adjacent sibling selectors) create predictable spacing and avoid edge-case bugs with last-child removal.

questions.question: Which property should you use for spacing INSIDE an element?
questions.explanation: Padding creates space inside an element (between content and border), while margin creates space outside.

questions.question: What happens when you set width: 100% plus margins on an element?
options: The element stays within its parent
The element overflows horizontally because margins add to the 100% width
questions.explanation: width: 100% makes the content area fill the parent, and margins add on top, causing horizontal overflow.

questions.question: What is the purpose of resetting default browser margins?
options: To remove all spacing from the page
To ensure consistent cross-browser layout by removing unpredictable defaults
questions.explanation: Browsers apply different default margins. A CSS reset ensures consistent spacing across all browsers.
